applied_optimizations.name: Fused Triton kernel with row-wise reduction
applied_optimizations.description: Implemented a single-pass Triton kernel that computes hinge loss elementwise and reduces per-row. Key optimizations: (1) Each block processes one row of the (32768, 32768) input, (2) Proper handling of 1D targets broadcasting across columns, (3) BLOCK_SIZE = 32768 (next_power_of_2 of n_cols) aligned with AMD wavefront size of 64, (4) Final reduction uses PyTorch's optimized sum. Achieved 7.08x speedup over PyTorch reference.

attempted_but_failed.name: Atomic add for global sum
attempted_but_failed.error: Memory access fault by GPU node-2

attempted_but_failed.name: Two-pass elementwise then mean
attempted_but_failed.error: Memory access fault by GPU node-2

attempted_but_failed.name: Incorrect broadcasting in kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Output mismatch - targets shape handling was wrong

notes: Key insight: The operation is memory-bound. The Triton kernel fuses the elementwise computation with per-row reduction, minimizing global memory traffic.
AMD-specific: BLOCK_SIZE should be multiple of 64 (wavefront size). Using triton.next_power_of_2(n_cols) = 32768 works well.
Broadcasting: targets is 1D (32768,) and broadcasts across columns. Loading target_val once per row is more efficient than broadcasting.
